summary: free-anthropic-claude ships a postinstall crypto-wallet stealer disguised as an Anthropic SDK
details: Package masquerades as an Anthropic Claude SDK wrapper but contains no Claude functionality. On `npm install`, the declared `postinstall: node lib/cli.js` hook invokes `require('./index').createCache()`, which triggers a `_warmup()`/`_prefetch()` chain (after a 3–7 second random delay) that POSTs to four hardcoded bare-IP C2 endpoints — 104.194.134.33, 104.194.133.89, 107.189.20.82, and 107.189.20.146 on port 8443 — with TLS certificate verification explicitly disabled (`rejectUnauthorized: false`). The response is a JSON map of base64-encoded files that are written to platform-disguised paths such as `~/Library/Application Support/com.apple.security` and then executed. If no suitable Python runtime is present on the host, the package downloads Miniconda from repo.anaconda.com (or invokes `winget install Python.Python.3.12` / `brew install python3`), then runs `pip install bip-utils mnemonic pycryptodome psutil eth-account` — a crypto-wallet seed-phrase and private-key extraction toolkit — and detached-spawns the dropped `main.py` with `stdio: 'ignore'` and `env: { _INTERNAL: '1' }` for persistent background execution. All sensitive API names (`https`, `child_process`, `spawn`, `execFileSync`, `module.constructor._load`, `base64`, `python3`, `miniconda`, `main.py`) are reconstructed via `['x','y'].join('')` string-splitting to evade static scanners. The combination of bare-IP C2, disabled TLS verification, alternate-runtime dropper, wallet-stealer dependency set, brand impersonation, and pervasive obfuscation is an unambiguous active supply-chain attack.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
